message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="heydon-pickering" color="cyan" summary="Success green fails for text, muted text borderline">
Lea, quick update from my contrast audit with Steve Schoger. One finding that affects the CSS tokens:

**`--color-success` (oklch 0.55 0.15 145) fails WCAG AA for normal text** at ~3.9:1. It passes for large text (3:1) and UI components.

The fix options:
1. Darken to L=0.50 or lower (would need Steve's approval on the visual)
2. Add a CSS comment restricting usage: "Use for icons and large indicators only. Not for normal-sized text."
3. Add a darker variant: `--color-success-dark: oklch(0.45 0.15 145)` for any text use

I'd recommend option 2 for now -- we're using green for completed-state indicators (checkmarks, strikethrough styling), not for text messages. Steve already confirmed completed todos will use strikethrough + color, not color alone.

Also, I've reiterated the `--color-fg-muted` recommendation: bump from gray-500 to gray-600. It's 4.6:1 right now -- technically passing but one rounding error away from failure. Gray-600 gives 7.4:1, which is comfortable.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Good findings from Heydon. I agree with the recommendations. Let me note these for when the stop lifts and respond.

input.content:
Good audit work. I agree with both recommendations:

**`--color-success`**: Option 2 is the right call. We're using green for visual indicators (checkmarks, completed state styling), not for body text. A CSS comment restricting usage is sufficient and avoids introducing a variant we don't need yet. If we ever need success-colored text, we add `--color-success-dark` at that point. YAGNI until then.

**`--color-fg-muted`**: Agreed -- bump to gray-600. A 4.6:1 ratio is technically AA but uncomfortable. Gray-600 at 7.4:1 gives us real headroom. There's no visual design reason to prefer the lighter gray; muted text should still be readable, just de-emphasized. The contrast bump achieves both.

I'll make both changes as separate commits once the stop directive lifts:
1. Add usage restriction comment to `--color-success`
2. Bump `--color-fg-muted` from gray-500 to gray-600

Plus the two from Steve's review (`:focus-visible` on skip link, generic `aria-invalid` base rule). Four small commits queued up.

Still standing by -- not touching files until CI on `6c757bc` is confirmed green and team-lead gives the go-ahead.
input.summary: Agree with both contrast fixes, queued for after stop

content.text:
Standing by. I have 4 CSS refinements queued for when the stop lifts:

1. Add usage restriction comment to `--color-success` (contrast issue for normal text)
2. Bump `--color-fg-muted` from gray-500 to gray-600 (contrast headroom)
3. Change skip link `:focus` to `:focus-visible`
4. Move `aria-invalid` border style to base layer (generic, not auth-form-scoped)

Not touching any files until team-lead confirms CI green and gives the go-ahead.
